name: Castelltallat Observatory Loop
name_original: Callus, observatorio astronómico, y Coll de Boixadors – vuelta por el Espacio Natural de la Sierra de Castellat
description: This moderate 29.6-mile road cycling loop from Callús explores the Sierra de Castellat, passing an astronomical observatory and Coll de Boix
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Callús are set within a diverse landscape in Catalonia, characterized by riverine areas along the Cardener river and elevated points. The region offers a mix of terrains suitable for road cycling, from flatter routes along riverbanks to ascents towards higher elevations like the Collbaix peak. Well-maintained roads with smooth tarmac and steady climbs are present, providing varied options for cyclists. This area combines natural features with historical and cultural landmarks, making it suitable for exploring on a road bike. Best road cycling routes around Callús The most popular road cycling route is Callus, astronomical observatory, and Coll de Boixadors – return through the Natural Space of the Sierra de Castellat, a 29.6 miles (47.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge with notable elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Convent de Sant Francesc – Ruins of the Calaf castle loop from FGC Súria (Mercaderies), a difficult 52.0 miles (83.8 km) path. This route features significant climbs and passes through varied terrain, including historical sites. Local road cyclists also love the Sant Sadurní Church (Callús) – Castillo de Cardona loop from Bages, a 39.4 miles (63.4 km) trail leading through the Bages region towards the historic Castillo de Cardona, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Road cycling around Callús is defined by river valleys, elevated points, and smooth tarmac roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with substantial elevation. The routes in Callús are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 11,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Callús's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of road cycling routes can I find around Callús?
A: Callús offers a diverse range of road cycling routes, from flatter paths along the Cardener river to more challenging ascents towards elevated points like the Collbaix peak. You'll find well-maintained roads with smooth tarmac and steady climbs, catering to various skill levels. Overall, there are nearly 200 road cycling routes in the area, with 13 easy, 48 moderate, and 138 difficult options.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Callús?
A: While many routes around Callús feature significant elevation, the region's riverine landscapes along the Cardener river suggest potential for flatter, less challenging rides. For specific family-friendly options, it's best to look for routes with minimal elevation gain and shorter distances, often found closer to the riverbanks.

Q: What are some popular road cycling routes in the Callús area?
A: One of the most popular routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995731265/callus-astronomical-observatory-and-coll-de-boixadors-return-through-the-natural-space-of-the-sierra-de-castellat" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Callus, astronomical observatory, and Coll de Boixadors – return through the Natural Space of the Sierra de Castellat&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 29.6-mile trail. Another favorite is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/11782339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Convent de Sant Francesc – Ruins of the Calaf castle loop from FGC Súria (Mercaderies)&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 52 miles and includes historical sites.

Q: What kind of elevation and terrain should I expect on road cycling routes near Callús?
A: The terrain around Callús is varied. You can expect routes with significant elevation changes, especially as you head towards higher points like the Collbaix peak. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40349978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelltallat Climb – Creu Grossa de Castelltallat loop from FGC Súria (Mercaderies)&lt;/a&gt; involves over 1800 meters of ascent. However, there are also flatter sections along the Cardener river for less strenuous rides.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see while road cycling around Callús?
A: The region is rich in historical and cultural sites. You can cycle past impressive structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4925300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basílica de Santa María de la Seu de Manresa&lt;/a&gt; and the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3826689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont Vell de Manresa&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20155979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Sadurní Church (Callús) – Castillo de Cardona loop from Bages&lt;/a&gt; will take you towards the historic Castillo de Cardona, offering a blend of cycling and sightseeing.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Callús?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20162288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Pinós – Geographical Center of Catalonia loop from Bages&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 46.7-mile route with over 1100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Callús?
A: The road cycling routes in Callús are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 11,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the well-maintained roads and diverse landscapes.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in the Callús region?
A: The broader Catalonia region, including Callús, generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there cafes or restaurants along the road cycling routes in Callús?
A: Callús is a welcoming village, and the surrounding towns like Manresa and Súria offer various amenities. While specific cafes directly on every route might vary, you'll find places to refuel and rest in the villages and towns you pass through. Planning your route with komoot can help identify points of interest for food and drink.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Callús?
A: Callús, as a village, typically offers public parking options. For routes starting from specific points, such as those near FGC Súria (Mercaderies), designated parking areas are usually available. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route for specific parking recommendations.

Q: Can I reach Callús by public transport with my bike?
A: The Bages region, where Callús is located, is accessible by public transport, including FGC (Ferrocarrils de la Generalitat de Catalunya) services to nearby towns like Súria. Policies regarding bikes on public transport can vary, so it's recommended to check with the specific transport provider for their current regulations on carrying bicycles.

Q: Are there any castles or historical sites to visit near Callús while cycling?
A: Absolutely. The area is dotted with historical castles. You can explore sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellnou Castle Tower&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1096822" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsareny Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/822739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rajadell Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The nearby town of Cardona, about 22 kilometers away, also boasts an imposing medieval fortress.
tips:
Located beside the local road BV-3003. The place is well marked with signs.

An astronomical observatory inaugurated in 2004, is located in the Sierra de Castelltallat in the municipality of Sant Mateu de Bages on a hill at 911 meters above sea level, where there are also the ruins of the castle of Montedono which has its origins in the seventh century.

The Serra de Castelltallat is a very depopulated place, there is only the presence of some farmhouses that make very little light at night, the situation of the Observatory, a thousand meters high, and isolated from any town, makes become a privileged place for sky observation.

It offers activities designed for all types of audiences. Website: https://astrocastellblog.wordpress.com/
Section with progressive ascents. At the top there is a bar with coffee and food.

Church of the municipality of Callús (Bages) included in the Inventory of the Architectural Heritage of Catalonia. This church was built in 1936 due to the fact that at the end of the 19th century a series of textile industries were installed on the banks of the Cardener River that were a pole of attraction for the population; Thus, a new town was set up on the plain that was far from the parish church located on top of a hill near the castle of Callús.

faqs.question: How long does this road cycling route typically take?
faqs.answer: This moderate road cycling loop, covering 47.6 kilometers, generally takes around 2 hours and 33 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: The route is categorized as 'moderate' and requires a good level of fitness. While much of it is well-paved, there are some unpaved and difficult segments where you might need to push your bike. It's not ideal for absolute beginners due to the elevation gain of nearly 790 meters and varied terrain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Callús. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you can typically find parking within the town of Callús to begin your ride. Consider looking for public parking near the starting point.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. You'll find mostly well-paved and easy-to-ride sections, which are great for road cycling. However, be prepared for some segments that are unpaved and more challenging, potentially requiring you to dismount and push your bike. This mix adds to the adventure and requires adaptability.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect beautiful landscapes as the route traverses the Natural Space of the Sierra de Castellat. Highlights include the Castelltallat Castle (astronomical observatory), offering panoramic views from 911 meters above sea level, and the incredible landscapes around Coll de Boixadors. The natural space is sparsely populated, ensuring a tranquil experience.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for cycling this route. The weather is usually milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be hot, and some unpaved sections might be more challenging after heavy rain in winter.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the Castelltallat Castle (astronomical observatory), which was inaugurated in 2004 and is a unique feature. You'll also encounter the challenging Castelltallat Climb and pass by the historic Sant Sadurní Church (Callús).

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it includes parts of the Ruta del romànic XVI.2 – Sant Mateu de Bages, Ruta del romànic XVI.3 – Sant Mateu de Bages, and the historic Camí Ral de Manresa a Cardona. You'll also briefly intersect with the GR 3.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route that includes some unpaved sections and passes through a natural space. While there are no explicit prohibitions against dogs, it's generally not recommended for dogs to accompany cyclists on a racebike route of this nature due to the speed, distance, and potential for challenging terrain. If you do bring a dog, ensure it is well-trained, on a leash, and capable of keeping up safely.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee for the Natural Space of the Sierra de Castellat?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the Natural Space of the Sierra de Castellat (Espai d'Interès Natural de la Serra de Castelltallat). Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling through natural spaces in this region, but always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice leave-no-trace principles.

faqs.question: What should I know about the astronomical observatory along the route?
faqs.answer: The astronomical observatory is located at the Castelltallat Castle, situated at 911 meters above sea level. It was inaugurated in 2004. The area is known for its minimal light pollution, making it ideal for stargazing. While you'll cycle past it, direct public access or visitor information for the observatory itself isn't typically part of the cycling experience, so check their official website if you plan a separate visit.
